title: Govinda Temple, Hojai

overview.short: Jagannath Govinda Temple is a Hindu temple in Gauranagar, in the Indian state of Assam which was established in 1928.

topPlaces.name: Komorakata
topPlaces.description: Komorakata (alternative spelling includes Kumurakata) is a village situated in Hojai district (formerly in Nagaon district) state of Assam, India. According to the 2011 Census of India, it has a population of 3,357.

topPlaces.name: Hojai
topPlaces.description: Hojai is a town and a municipal board in Hojai district in the Indian state of Assam. It is located on the banks of the two tributaries of Brahmaputra, namely Kapili and Jamuna.

topPlaces.name: Kolong river
topPlaces.description: The Kolong River or Kailang is an anabranch of the Brahmaputra River, which diverts out from the Brahamputra river in Hatimura region of Jakhalabandha (Nagaon district, Assam, India), and meets the same at Kolongpar near Guwahati. The tributary is about 250 kilometres (160 mi) long and flows through the districts of Nagaon, Morigaon and Kamrup.

topPlaces.name: Lanka (town)
topPlaces.description: Lanka is a town located in Hojai District of Assam, and a municipal board with 11 wards. It serves as a trade and commerce hub for nearby areas such as Udali, Nokhuti, Laskar Pather, Karikhana, Kheroni, Dablong, and Doiyong sides.

items.note: Komorakata (alternative spelling includes Kumurakata) is a village situated in Hojai district (formerly in Nagaon district) state of Assam, India. According to the 2011 Census of India, it has a population of 3,357.
Hojai is a town and a municipal board in Hojai district in the Indian state of Assam. It is located on the banks of the two tributaries of Brahmaputra, namely Kapili and Jamuna.

The Kolong River or Kailang is an anabranch of the Brahmaputra River, which diverts out from the Brahamputra river in Hatimura region of Jakhalabandha (Nagaon district, Assam, India), and meets the same at Kolongpar near Guwahati. The tributary is about 250 kilometres (160 mi) long and flows through the districts of Nagaon, Morigaon and Kamrup.

Lanka is a town located in Hojai District of Assam, and a municipal board with 11 wards. It serves as a trade and commerce hub for nearby areas such as Udali, Nokhuti, Laskar Pather, Karikhana, Kheroni, Dablong, and Doiyong sides.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Govinda Temple, Hojai?
faq.a: The best time to visit Govinda Temple, Hojai is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Govinda Temple, Hojai?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dimapur Airport (90 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (86 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Govinda Temple, Hojai?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Govinda Temple, Hojai at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Govinda Temple, Hojai?
faq.a: Stays in Govinda Temple, Hojai start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Govinda Temple, Hojai?
faq.a: The top attractions are Komorakata, Hojai, Kolong river, Lanka (town).
